user_query:
I'm shortlisting explainability techniques for tree-based models (random forests and gradient-boosted trees) on tabular data for my ML interpretability research. Using only the provided local inputs, please consolidate, filter, and rank methods from both the offline HTML snapshot and the CSV catalog, grounded by my preferences in the notes. Requirements:

Inputs to use:
- input/webpage/interpretability_methods.html (offline HTML table of methods)
- input/catalog/methods_catalog.csv (CSV catalog of methods)
- input/notes/preferences.md (my filtering criteria, scoring weights, and tie-breakers)

Normalize and merge:
- Extract the methods table from the HTML and read the CSV, normalizing to these fields: method_name, explanation_type, approach, model_compatibility, compute_cost, faithfulness, open_source.
- Merge records case-insensitively on method_name and keep only methods that appear in BOTH sources to ensure cross-validation.

Apply my preferences and scoring from input/notes/preferences.md:
- Filters (must match all): explanation_type == local; compute_cost != high; open_source == Yes; model_compatibility is considered tree-compatible only if it is "model-agnostic" or "tree-only".
- Scoring (compute a numeric score for each remaining method): use the weights and mappings in the notes file, including the tie-breaker order provided there. Use the exact mapping values for faithfulness and compute efficiency from the notes.

Deliverables (write all artifacts to these paths):
1) output/normalized/methods_merged.csv — the merged, cross-validated methods with columns: method_name, explanation_type, approach, model_compatibility, compute_cost, faithfulness, open_source, sources (comma-separated list of sources used), computed_score (integer). Include all methods that survive the merge, before filtering. Then also produce a filtered view in the same file by appending only those that pass all filters below the merged section, separated by a blank line and a single header row repeated for clarity.
2) output/filtered/top3_methods.json — an array of the top 3 methods after filters and scoring, ordered from highest score to lowest, applying tie-breakers exactly as specified in the notes. Each item must include: method_name, computed_score, and a short why string that cites at least three attributes (e.g., explanation_type, model_compatibility, compute_cost, faithfulness, open_source).
3) output/reports/consistency_report.tsv — a TSV with columns: method_name, in_html (true/false), in_csv (true/false), indicating presence in each source for all unique method names found across both inputs.
4) output/parsed/preferences_extracted.yaml — a machine-readable extraction of the filters, weights, mappings, and tie-breaker order you used from the notes file. Reflect the exact values you applied.

Please do not use the internet. The results should be fully derivable from the three inputs. Make sure your ranking and filters are directly traceable to the values in the inputs, and that the tie-breaking is deterministic. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
